topics.title: GSC opportunity review: Last Z Redeem Codes — Active Codes, Gift Center Login, and UID
topics.source_reference: GSC weekly 2026-05-03: page opportunity and query-page signals
topics.notes: High impression volume plus multiple low-CTR gift center queries make codes.html a strong candidate for human review. The page already has backlog history and clear canonical protections, so the opportunity is to improve query match and first-screen usefulness without altering cluster role separation. Prior run `2026-05-05-codes-gsc-opportunity-llm-approved-intake` is `closed`; keep this topic in monitoring unless new evidence materially changes the opportunity.
topics.player_value: Better match for gift center and redeem-intent searches should reduce friction for players trying to redeem codes or locate the correct flow.
topics.duplication_risk: Medium; must avoid overlapping with mailbox or login guidance and keep redeem flow canonical.
topics.next_step: Send to human owner review for a scoped update plan on codes.html, with explicit checks against canonical claim boundaries.
evidence: GSC page signal: codes.html had 24154 impressions, 503 clicks, 2.08% CTR, avg position 6.46.
Low CTR query: `last z gift center` had 3048 impressions, 104 clicks, 3.41% CTR, position 6.98.
Low CTR query: `last z gift center login` had 959 impressions, 44 clicks, 4.59% CTR, position 5.56.
Low CTR query: `lastz gift center` had 501 impressions, 20 clicks, 3.99% CTR, position 6.89.
Low CTR query: `last-z.com gift center` had 472 impressions, 20 clicks, 4.24% CTR, position 6.22.

closeout.note: Owner-reviewed no-op proposal. Current codes.html first-screen and metadata already match the intended codes hub role; no content change applied.

topics.notes: The home page has a broad query footprint and rising generic interest in last z terms. Because it is a hub page, small navigation or intro refinements could improve entry-point relevance without creating new content.
